paper_map.title: Factors influencing austenite stability during Inter-critical annealing and effect on mechanical properties of low-density medium Mn steels
paper_map.abstract_summary: The study investigates the effect of adding 3% Al with and without 1% Si to an Fe-8Mn-0.2C medium-Mn steel on microstructure and mechanical properties after intercritical annealing (IA) and water quenching. Si-free steel achieved 45% retained austenite (RA) and a product of strength and elongation (PSE) of 61 GPa%, outperforming Si-containing steel (19% RA, 41 GPa%). Superior performance is attributed to higher RA fraction and slower TRIP kinetics, while thermodynamic calculations and local heterogeneity factors (element partitioning, grain size) explain enhanced austenite stability.
paper_map.research_objective: To elucidate the influence of Al and Si additions on austenite stability during intercritical annealing of a medium-Mn low-density steel and its correlation with the strength–ductility combination.
material_systems: phases: α-ferrite, δ-ferrite, retained austenite, martensite
main_process_variables: Intercritical annealing temperature (600–688 °C, broadened by Al/Si)
key_sections: Introduction: background on low-density steels, medium-Mn TRIP steels, and motivation for studying Al/Si addition and austenite stability
Alloy design (in Materials and Methods): thermodynamic predictions using Thermo-Calc to select two compositions
Microstructural evolution during hot-rolling: initial microstructure before IA
Microstructural evolution during intercritical annealing: effect of IA on phase formation (ferrite, austenite) using EBSD and XRD
Factors affecting austenite stability during IA: role of IA temperature, thermodynamic predictions, and Koistinen-Marburger model
Partitioning and local enrichment of Mn: Mn diffusion and heterogeneity factors
Mechanical properties of hot-rolled and IA samples: tensile results, PSE comparison, and retained austenite fractions
Effect of austenite stability on deformation mechanisms: TRIP kinetics, mechanical stability parameter, and work-hardening analysis
Conclusions: summary of key findings on composition effects and mechanism
